title: Vercel Blob tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Vercel Blob storage token detected in assignment. This pattern identifies Vercel Blob storage tokens when assigned to variables, configuration settings, or environment variables. Vercel Blob tokens provide comprehensive access to blob storage operations, file management, and data storage services within the Vercel platform ecosystem. These tokens enable critical storage operations including uploading files, images, documents, and binary data to Vercel's global blob storage infrastructure, downloading and serving stored content through CDN endpoints with optimized performance and caching, managing blob metadata including file sizes, content types, creation timestamps, and custom properties, configuring access permissions and sharing settings for stored blobs and data objects, accessing detailed storage usage analytics including bandwidth consumption, storage quotas, and access patterns, organizing blobs into hierarchical structures and implementing content organization strategies, configuring automatic content optimization, compression, and format conversion for web delivery, managing storage lifecycle policies including automatic deletion and archival of expired content, accessing blob storage APIs for programmatic file operations and integration with applications, and controlling cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) policies for browser-based blob access and content delivery. When a Vercel Blob token is compromised, attackers can gain unauthorized access to your entire blob storage infrastructure to upload malicious files including executable malware, phishing pages, illegal content, or inappropriate material under your account, download and exfiltrate all stored files including sensitive documents, customer data, business records, and proprietary content, delete or corrupt critical stored data causing permanent loss of important files and business-critical information, modify blob metadata and configurations to disrupt content delivery and application functionality, access storage usage analytics revealing business patterns, traffic data, and operational intelligence, upload massive amounts of data to exhaust storage quotas and generate unexpected billing charges, serve malicious content through your CDN endpoints potentially compromising user devices and spreading malware, configure malicious CORS policies to enable cross-site attacks and unauthorized data access, use your storage infrastructure as a hosting platform for illegal activities or copyright infringement, access and manipulate lifecycle policies to prevent legitimate data archival or cause premature content deletion, inject malicious code into stored files that could compromise applications accessing the blob storage, redirect blob URLs to malicious endpoints for phishing or social engineering attacks, and abuse your storage reputation and infrastructure to conduct distributed attacks or host command-and-control servers. The security and business consequences can be devastating including massive data breaches exposing confidential files, customer documents, and sensitive business information with severe regulatory penalties, significant financial losses from storage abuse, bandwidth overages, incident response costs, and potential legal liabilities from data exposure, catastrophic reputational damage from malicious content served through your infrastructure affecting customer trust and brand integrity, operational disruption from deleted or corrupted files causing application failures and service outages, compliance violations under data protection and content regulations requiring extensive investigation and remediation, loss of intellectual property through unauthorized access to proprietary documents, designs, and business materials, legal liability from copyright infringement or illegal content hosted on your storage infrastructure, customer data privacy violations affecting personal files, images, and sensitive documents stored in blob storage, supply chain security risks if compromised storage is used to distribute malware through legitimate application downloads, and permanent damage to business relationships from security incidents affecting customer and partner confidence in your data handling practices. Take immediate emergency action by accessing your Vercel dashboard security settings, navigating to the blob storage or API tokens management section, identifying and immediately revoking all potentially compromised blob storage tokens and credentials, generating new secure blob tokens with minimal required permissions and well-defined access scopes, updating all applications, storage clients, upload handlers, and automated systems with the new secure credentials, conducting a comprehensive audit of all blob storage access logs to identify unauthorized uploads, downloads, or modifications during the exposure period, scanning all stored files for malicious content including executable files, suspicious documents, and inappropriate material, reviewing blob metadata and configurations for unauthorized changes to permissions, CORS settings, or lifecycle policies, checking storage usage reports and billing statements for unexpected spikes in usage indicating malicious activity, validating the integrity and authenticity of all critical stored files and restoring from secure backups if necessary, examining CDN access logs for suspicious content delivery patterns or malicious file serving activities, reviewing and updating access permissions to ensure proper security controls and least-privilege access principles, implementing enhanced security monitoring and alerting for all blob storage operations and access attempts, migrating blob token storage to secure environment variables, encrypted configuration files, or enterprise key management systems, establishing comprehensive logging and real-time monitoring for all file uploads, downloads, and storage operations, implementing automated content scanning and malware detection for all uploaded files, providing targeted security training for development teams on proper blob storage security and token management, documenting the complete incident timeline and response actions for regulatory compliance and security auditing, and engaging cybersecurity professionals to conduct thorough penetration testing and implement advanced threat detection capabilities for your blob storage infrastructure.

Vercel Blob storage token with specific prefix detected. This pattern identifies Vercel Blob tokens that follow the standard 'vercel_blob_' prefix format used by Vercel's blob storage service for programmatic access to cloud storage resources. Vercel Blob tokens with this prefix provide comprehensive blob storage capabilities including high-performance file uploads to globally distributed storage infrastructure, optimized content delivery through Vercel's edge network and CDN integration, advanced file management operations including metadata manipulation and batch processing, secure URL generation for temporary and permanent file access with configurable expiration policies, storage analytics and usage monitoring for capacity planning and cost optimization, automatic content optimization including image resizing, format conversion, and compression for web delivery, integration with Vercel's serverless functions for dynamic file processing and transformation workflows, cross-region replication and backup capabilities for data redundancy and disaster recovery, fine-grained access control and permission management for multi-tenant applications and team collaboration, and seamless integration with deployment pipelines for static asset management and build artifact storage. When these prefixed blob tokens are exposed in source code repositories, configuration files, or documentation, attackers can exploit them to compromise your complete blob storage ecosystem for unauthorized file uploads including malware, ransomware, phishing content, and illegal material hosted under your account, massive data exfiltration of all stored files including customer uploads, business documents, application assets, and sensitive information, destructive operations including bulk file deletion, corruption of stored data, and manipulation of storage configurations, storage infrastructure abuse through excessive uploads designed to exhaust quotas and generate massive billing charges, content delivery network poisoning by uploading malicious files that are served to legitimate users through your CDN endpoints, metadata manipulation to hide malicious activities or corrupt file organization systems, unauthorized access to storage analytics revealing business patterns, user behavior data, and operational intelligence, abuse of automatic optimization features to process and transform malicious content through your infrastructure, exploitation of serverless function integrations to execute malicious code in your deployment environment, manipulation of access controls to create backdoors for persistent unauthorized access to storage resources, and use of your storage infrastructure as a distribution platform for malware, pirated content, or illegal activities. The resulting impact can be catastrophic including complete compromise of your cloud storage infrastructure leading to massive data breaches and regulatory fines, severe financial losses from storage abuse, bandwidth overages, premium feature exploitation, and extensive incident response costs, devastating reputational damage from malicious content distribution through your trusted domains and CDN infrastructure, operational paralysis from storage outages, corrupted files, and disrupted content delivery affecting user experience, exposure of confidential business data, customer information, and intellectual property to malicious actors and competitors, regulatory compliance violations under data protection laws requiring comprehensive investigation, notification, and remediation efforts, legal liability from copyright infringement, illegal content hosting, or privacy violations affecting customer and user data, permanent loss of customer trust and business relationships from security incidents affecting data integrity and service reliability, supply chain security compromise if your storage is used to distribute compromised software or malicious updates, and long-term business impact from damaged reputation requiring extensive recovery efforts and customer rebuilding initiatives. Respond with immediate emergency measures by accessing your Vercel account dashboard through secure authentication, navigating to blob storage settings and API token management interfaces, identifying and instantly revoking all compromised blob tokens including any with the 'vercel_blob_' prefix pattern, generating new secure blob storage tokens with strict permission scopes and time-limited expiration policies, updating all applications, storage clients, file upload systems, and automated processes with the new secure credentials using encrypted distribution methods, conducting exhaustive forensic analysis of all blob storage access logs to identify every unauthorized operation during the token exposure window, performing comprehensive security scans of all stored files to detect malicious uploads, executable content, and suspicious material, reviewing and validating all blob metadata, access permissions, and storage configurations for unauthorized modifications, analyzing CDN and content delivery logs for evidence of malicious file serving or suspicious access patterns, checking storage utilization reports and billing statements for indicators of abuse or unexpected resource consumption, implementing immediate access restrictions and enhanced monitoring for all blob storage operations and file transfers, restoring critical data from secure, verified backups and validating integrity of all restored content, implementing automated malware scanning and content filtering for all new uploads and existing stored files, migrating all blob token storage to enterprise-grade secret management systems with comprehensive audit trails, establishing real-time security monitoring and automated threat detection for storage access patterns and file operations, implementing multi-factor authentication and IP-based access controls for storage management operations, conducting comprehensive security training for all development and operations teams on blob storage security best practices, documenting the complete incident response timeline and affected systems for regulatory compliance and forensic analysis, and engaging specialized cybersecurity experts to perform penetration testing and implement advanced security controls for your blob storage infrastructure.

Vercel Blob storage token detected. This pattern identifies potentially sensitive alphanumeric strings that may represent Vercel Blob storage access tokens, authentication credentials, or storage API keys used for programmatic access to blob storage services. Vercel Blob storage tokens are typically longer alphanumeric strings that provide authenticated access to cloud storage infrastructure, file management APIs, and content delivery networks within the Vercel platform ecosystem. These tokens enable comprehensive storage operations including secure file uploads to distributed cloud storage with automatic redundancy and backup, high-performance downloads through global CDN endpoints optimized for speed and reliability, advanced metadata management for file organization, tagging, and search capabilities, content transformation and optimization including image resizing, format conversion, and compression, access control configuration for public and private file sharing with granular permissions, storage analytics and monitoring for usage tracking, capacity planning, and cost optimization, integration with serverless functions for dynamic file processing and real-time content manipulation, lifecycle management including automated archival, deletion, and retention policy enforcement, cross-region replication for disaster recovery and improved global access performance, and seamless integration with deployment workflows for static asset management and build optimization. When these blob storage tokens are compromised through exposure in source code, configuration files, or insecure storage systems, attackers can exploit them to gain complete control over your blob storage infrastructure for unauthorized data operations and malicious activities including massive file uploads of malware, ransomware, phishing content, illegal material, or resource-exhausting junk data, comprehensive data exfiltration of all stored files including customer uploads, business documents, proprietary content, and sensitive information, destructive operations such as mass file deletion, data corruption, or metadata manipulation causing permanent loss of critical information, storage quota abuse through excessive uploads designed to generate unexpected billing charges and exhaust available resources, content delivery network poisoning by uploading malicious files that are automatically served to legitimate users through your CDN infrastructure, manipulation of access controls and sharing permissions to create unauthorized backdoors or expose private content publicly, exploitation of content transformation features to process malicious payloads through your infrastructure, abuse of analytics access to gather intelligence about your business operations, user patterns, and technical infrastructure, integration exploitation to execute malicious code through serverless function triggers and automated processing pipelines, and use of your storage reputation and infrastructure to host and distribute illegal content, malware, or conduct cyber attacks against third parties. The security and business consequences can be severe including catastrophic data breaches exposing all stored files and sensitive information with resulting regulatory fines and legal liability, massive financial losses from storage abuse, bandwidth overage charges, premium feature exploitation, and comprehensive incident response costs, severe reputational damage from malicious content served through your infrastructure affecting customer trust and business relationships, operational disruption from storage outages, corrupted files, deleted content, and disrupted applications dependent on blob storage, exposure of intellectual property, customer data, and confidential business information to competitors and malicious actors, compliance violations under data protection regulations requiring extensive investigation, notification, and remediation efforts, legal consequences from hosting illegal content or facilitating cyber crimes through your compromised infrastructure, permanent loss of customer confidence affecting business growth and market position, supply chain security risks if your storage is used to distribute compromised software or malicious updates to downstream users, and long-term reputation recovery challenges requiring extensive public relations efforts and customer rebuilding initiatives. Take immediate comprehensive action by securing access to your Vercel account through verified authentication methods, navigating to blob storage and security settings in the dashboard, identifying and immediately revoking all suspicious or potentially compromised storage tokens and credentials, generating new secure blob storage tokens with minimal required permissions and time-limited expiration dates, updating all client applications, upload systems, content management tools, and automated processes with new secure credentials, conducting thorough forensic analysis of storage access logs and audit trails to identify all unauthorized activities during the exposure period, performing comprehensive security scanning of all stored files to detect malicious uploads, executable content, and inappropriate material, reviewing all storage configurations, access permissions, and sharing settings for unauthorized modifications, analyzing content delivery logs and CDN access patterns for evidence of malicious file serving, checking storage usage reports and billing statements for signs of abuse or unexpected resource consumption, implementing enhanced security controls including IP-based access restrictions, rate limiting, and enhanced monitoring, restoring critical data from secure, verified backup sources and validating integrity of all restored files, implementing automated content scanning and malware detection for all current and future file uploads, migrating token storage to enterprise-grade secret management systems with comprehensive encryption and access controls, establishing real-time security monitoring and automated alerting for all storage operations and access attempts, implementing multi-factor authentication requirements for storage administration and high-privilege operations, providing comprehensive security awareness training for all team members handling blob storage credentials and operations, documenting the complete security incident timeline and response actions for compliance reporting and audit purposes, and engaging qualified cybersecurity professionals to conduct penetration testing, security assessments, and implement advanced threat detection capabilities for your blob storage infrastructure.

Vercel Blob storage authorization header detected. This pattern identifies Vercel Blob storage authentication credentials passed through HTTP headers including custom authorization headers, Blob-specific authentication tokens, and storage API access headers. Authentication headers are critical security mechanisms that provide access control to Vercel's blob storage APIs, file management endpoints, content delivery services, and storage administration interfaces. These headers typically contain sensitive authentication material such as blob storage access tokens providing full read/write access to stored files and storage management operations, personal blob tokens enabling user-specific storage permissions and quota management, team-level storage tokens granting collaborative access to shared storage resources and organizational blob management, temporary upload tokens allowing time-limited file upload operations with restricted permissions and expiration controls, download authentication tokens enabling secure access to private files and protected content delivery, storage administration tokens providing access to storage configuration, analytics, and billing information, CDN authentication headers enabling optimized content delivery and caching control through Vercel's edge network, and integration tokens facilitating secure communication between storage APIs and external services or applications. When these blob storage authentication headers are exposed in source code, server logs, configuration files, or documentation, attackers can intercept and misuse them to perform unauthorized storage operations including massive file uploads of malicious content, malware, ransomware, or illegal material to your blob storage infrastructure, comprehensive data exfiltration of all stored files including customer uploads, business documents, media assets, and sensitive information, destructive file operations including mass deletion, corruption of stored data, and manipulation of file metadata and organization structures, storage resource abuse through excessive uploads designed to exhaust quotas, generate unexpected billing charges, and disrupt service availability, unauthorized access to private files and protected content through compromised download authentication, manipulation of storage configurations including access permissions, sharing settings, and lifecycle policies, abuse of CDN integration to serve malicious content through your trusted domain and content delivery infrastructure, access to storage analytics and usage data revealing business patterns, user behavior, and operational intelligence, exploitation of temporary tokens to maintain persistent unauthorized access beyond intended expiration periods, and use of administrative tokens to modify billing settings, add malicious users, or reconfigure storage security controls. The security and business impact can be catastrophic including complete compromise of blob storage infrastructure leading to massive data breaches and regulatory penalties, severe financial losses from storage abuse, bandwidth overages, incident response costs, and potential legal liabilities, devastating reputational damage from malicious content served through your infrastructure affecting customer trust and business relationships, operational disruption from storage outages, corrupted files, and disrupted content delivery affecting application functionality and user experience, exposure of confidential business data, customer information, and intellectual property stored in blob storage to malicious actors and competitors, regulatory compliance violations under data protection and privacy laws requiring extensive investigation and remediation, legal liability from hosting illegal content, copyright infringement, or privacy violations through compromised storage access, permanent erosion of customer trust and confidence affecting long-term business growth and market position, supply chain security risks if your storage is used to distribute compromised content or malicious updates to downstream applications, and extensive reputation recovery efforts requiring significant resources and time to rebuild customer and partner confidence. Take immediate emergency response action by securing access to your Vercel account dashboard through verified authentication methods, navigating to blob storage security settings and API authentication management, identifying and immediately revoking all potentially compromised authentication headers and storage tokens, generating new secure authentication credentials with minimal required permissions and well-defined access scopes, updating all client applications, storage integration systems, CDN configurations, and automated processes with new secure headers using encrypted distribution, conducting comprehensive analysis of storage access logs and audit trails to identify all unauthorized operations performed using compromised headers, performing thorough security scans of all stored files to detect malicious uploads, inappropriate content, and suspicious material, reviewing storage configurations, access permissions, and sharing settings for unauthorized modifications made during the compromise period, analyzing CDN access logs and content delivery patterns for evidence of malicious file serving or suspicious distribution activities, checking storage utilization reports and billing statements for indicators of abuse, unexpected usage spikes, or premium feature exploitation, implementing enhanced security controls including IP address restrictions, rate limiting, and real-time monitoring for all storage operations, restoring critical data from secure, verified backup sources and validating integrity of all restored files and metadata, implementing automated content scanning, malware detection, and policy enforcement for all uploaded files, migrating authentication header storage to enterprise-grade secret management systems with comprehensive encryption and audit capabilities, establishing real-time security monitoring and automated threat detection for all storage access attempts and file operations, implementing multi-factor authentication requirements and IP-based access controls for storage administration, providing targeted security awareness training for all development and operations teams on proper blob storage authentication and security practices, documenting the complete incident response timeline and affected systems for regulatory compliance and security auditing, and engaging certified cybersecurity professionals to conduct comprehensive penetration testing and implement advanced security measures for your blob storage authentication and access control systems.
